what_it_does: A research-finding doc on the REDUCE stage of the corpus-chain: the strong layer that joins, adjudicates/verifies, composes, and decides-next. It reads the real engine end-to-end, measures the staging threshold against the real corpus and context windows, and concludes the reduce is the least-built stage, specifying what it must do, what it can reuse, and the one net-new seam it is blocked on.
observations: Companion to CORPUS-CHAIN-ANCHOR.md; uses an OBS/VERIFIED/INF/EXT/idea evidence template grounded in file:lines (cognition.py, suite.py, roles/, verify_jury.py, services.json, transport.py).

Headline finding: the MAP (run_swarm) is proven but the REDUCE's central job, cross-unit JOIN, has no mechanism in the code; the reduce-tree archetype (suite.py:1474) is declared-but-dead (join/render_from never read at runtime, no reduce role file).

Argues run_jury is not the JOIN: it is N draws of one role on one input (intra-unit variance), and same-model draws cannot catch a systematically-wrong extraction (citing verify_jury.py:12-18 and SEM-3's keystone).
Notes what the live system calls 'reduce' is the deterministic INJECTION_RULE (cognition.py:160), routing a pre-computed field, not strong-model synthesis across units.
Identifies the blocking net-new seam shared with SEM-1: generalize run_role to build messages from role.input_addresses instead of the hardcoded ctx['utterance'].

Measures staging numbers: coherence-scan {contradicts,note} ~50-150 tok/unit (thousands fit one call); research-wave companions ~9-10K tok each (six already ~60K, overflowing the local synth), framing staging as a compile-time computation n_units x est_digest_tokens(map_schema) vs synth_context.

Argues hierarchical reduce is in tension with the JOIN (cross-cluster pairs meet only at summarized ancestors) and that clustering must be query-dependent, with ToM-style confidence-carrying structured ascent rather than lossy prose.
Frames decide-next as a declared rule over the reduce's status field (bounded by the compiler's pass-plan, run by AREA-4's loop), explicitly rejecting an agentic controller as the wrong idiom.
Proposes making followup-routing fail-safe by folding it into the adjudicate gate: no citable digest support implies a targeted re-map.
Cites external prior art (RAPTOR, ToM/Tree-oriented MapReduce, LLMxMapReduce, LangChain collapse, agentic RAG, Self-RAG).
standouts: The grounded claim that the reduce is the least-built stage and that its core JOIN has no engine behind it, with grep-level evidence that reduce-tree's keys are never read at runtime.
Reframing the staging decision as compile-time-derived from map_schema x unit-count vs synth context, surfaceable at the inspect-approve gate before any tokens spend.
The query-dependent clustering argument: similarity-cluster helps built-twice, structural-pairing sidesteps the tree for half-migration, generic RAPTOR only for join-free faces.
Citation-as-router: making the reduce ground its answer in named units turns followup-routing into a fail-safe byproduct of adjudication rather than a fragile classifier.
conventions: Follows the repo evidence-tagging discipline and the verdict-first / yes-but-actually correction-list structure.
Composes over existing primitives rather than inventing orchestration, and explicitly honors the Company's not-agent-architecture-by-default principle.
Lists external sources at the end.
concerns: Several digest-size figures are marked VERIFIED-estimated rather than fully measured per-face, so the exact staging thresholds are approximate.
notes: A grounded REDUCE-stage analysis arguing the reduce (JOIN/adjudicate/compose/decide-next) is the unbuilt heavy lifter, blocked on the same run_role input-seam SEM-1 named, with a config-derived single/hierarchical/query-loop shape decidable at compile time.
questions: What schema should the reduce/join and compose role files declare as their output shape for each face?
Where does the query-dependent clustering policy live and who emits it (the compiler) versus who executes it?
purpose_vs_actual: Match. The doc's allocated area is the reduce engine and it delivers a job-by-job build-state assessment, measured thresholds, and an ordered net-new build list.
apparent_themes: The reduce as the strong/expensive layer in a cheap-map/expensive-reduce cost shape
Cross-unit reasoning (JOIN) as the value the map cannot provide
Rejecting agent-as-default in favor of declared rules over model outputs
intention_signals: It is trying to redirect the build effort to where it is actually missing, proving with grep-level evidence that the reduce is unbuilt, and specifying a minimal-delta path (one real engine seam plus thin compositions) so the corpus-chain's heavy lifter becomes real without importing the agentic idioms the Company rejects.
novelty: The compile-time staging predictor formula, the JOIN-vs-clustering tension analysis with query-dependent clustering, and citation-absence-as-remap-signal.
contribution: Contributes a job-by-job build-state map of the reduce (JOIN/adjudicate/compose/decide-next), the grep-grounded finding that the reduce-tree archetype is dead and run_jury is not a join, the shared run_role input-seam, a measured compile-time staging formula, the query-dependent clustering and ToM-style ascent design, the cross-tier adjudicate panel, decide-next as a declared rule over a status field, and the fail-safe citation-as-router mechanism, plus an ordered net-new build list.
summary_for_embedding: A grounded research finding (CC-4) showing the corpus-chain REDUCE stage is the least-built part of the engine (cross-unit JOIN has no mechanism, reduce-tree is declared-but-dead, run_jury is not a join), and specifying a config-derived single/hierarchical/query-loop reduce reusing existing primitives plus one shared run_role input-seam.
intention_for_embedding: To redirect the corpus-chain build to the unbuilt reduce stage and specify, with measured thresholds and minimal-delta reuse, how the strong join/adjudicate/compose/decide-next layer should be built.
